headline: JPMorgan Deepens Blockchain Bet As Tokenization Meets Valuation Opportunity
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon is backing a stronger push into blockchain, tokenization, and crypto related activity. The bank is rebranding its Onyx platform, expanding the use of JPM Coin, and supporting a tokenized money market fund on Ethereum. These moves point to a clearer role for blockchain based products within JPMorgan Chase's broader digital strategy. For a bank of JPMorgan Chase's size and influence, a clearer embrace of blockchain and tokenization matters for how...

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, American, Oracle, Novo Nordisk, Bloom Energy, Globalstar, JPMorgan, and More
summary: Stock futures were rising Tuesday as investors’ hopes for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran strengthened.  Intel  climbed 1.4%, putting the chip maker on course to extend its winning streak to 10 sessions.  Shares have jumped 58% over the past nine trading days, rallying thanks to collaborations with  Tesla  and  Alphabet  Google, and Intel buying back full control of its chip factory in Ireland.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, BlackRock, American, Credo, CarMax, J&J, and More
summary: Stock futures were mostly higher Tuesday as investors gathered hope for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran and big banks ushered in the earnings season.  First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

content.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, BlackRock, American, Credo, CarMax, J&J, and More
content.summary: Stock futures were mostly higher Tuesday as investors gathered hope for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran and big banks ushered in the earnings season.  First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

headline: JPMorgan Traders Notch Record Revenue During Volatile Quarter
summary: A volatile first quarter for assets of all stripes—stocks, bonds, commodities—lifted JPMorgan’s trading business to a record.  The bank said its markets revenue jumped by 20% from a year ago to $11.6 billion, an all-time high.  JPMorgan’s markets business, housed in the firm’s commercial and investment bank, reported that fixed-income markets revenue rose by 21% to $7.1 billion.

content.title: JPMorgan Traders Notch Record Revenue During Volatile Quarter
content.summary: A volatile first quarter for assets of all stripes—stocks, bonds, commodities—lifted JPMorgan’s trading business to a record.  The bank said its markets revenue jumped by 20% from a year ago to $11.6 billion, an all-time high.  JPMorgan’s markets business, housed in the firm’s commercial and investment bank, reported that fixed-income markets revenue rose by 21% to $7.1 billion.

summary: Global financial services giant JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 9.8% year on year to $50.54 billion. Its GAAP profit of $5.94 per share was 8.9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
